text.title: Influencer Marketing Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Influencer Marketing` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Default partner tier MUST be micro (10k-100k followers) for first 6 months. Mega (>1M) only with documented ROI from prior micro tier.

rationale.source: Influencer Marketing Hub benchmarks 2024; HypeAuditor data

rule.rationale: Micro-influencer engagement rate is 3-5x mega's. Higher engagement = higher conversion. Mega tier requires brand-awareness budget, not direct-response budget — different goal entirely.

rule.statement: Every partnership MUST be vetted on (a) audience demographic match to ICP + (b) engagement-rate >= 2% + (c) fraud signals (bot ratio < 15%). Reject below threshold.

rationale.source: HypeAuditor / Modash vetting framework; FTC fake-follower casework

rule.rationale: Big-follower-low-engagement creators send tire-kickers; high-bot creators send fakes. Three-gate vetting (match + engagement + fraud) filters down to bookable partners.

rule.statement: Outreach MUST reference specific recent content (>= 1 piece) + tailored value prop. Blast templates ('Hi {Name}, we love your content') forbidden — 1-3% reply rate vs 15-25% personalized.
rule.rationale: Generic blast feels transactional; creators delete. Personalized references signal you actually consumed work + understand their brand. Reply rates 5-10x.

rule.statement: Every partnership MUST include FTC-compliant #ad / #sponsored / 'paid partnership' disclosure in writing + audit >= 20% placements.

rationale.source: FTC 16 CFR Part 255; Endorsement Guides 2023 update

rule.rationale: Undisclosed paid posts violate FTC; brand penalized along with creator. Explicit disclosure clause + audit creates the paper trail + protects the brand.

rule.statement: Every partnership MUST have isolated tracking (unique UTM / promo code / dedicated link) + 90-day attribution window. Aggregate-only tracking forbidden.

rationale.source: tracking ops best-practice; Tapfiliate / Aspire docs

rule.rationale: Without per-partner isolation, brand can't tell which partners convert. Aggregate roll-ups conceal good vs bad partners; tracking surfaces who to renew.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
